solution.answer: $H^*(T^2;\,\mathbb{Z}) \cong \Lambda_{\mathbb{Z}}[a,b] = \mathbb{Z}\langle 1, a, b, a \cup b\rangle,\quad |a| = |b| = 1,\quad a^2 = b^2 = 0,\quad a \cup b = -b \cup a.$
steps: Additively: $H^0 = \mathbb{Z}$, $H^1 = \mathbb{Z}^2$ (with generators $a, b$ dual to $[S^1 \times \{*\}]$ and $[\{*\} \times S^1]$), $H^2 = \mathbb{Z}$.
Graded commutativity of cup product: $\alpha \cup \beta = (-1)^{pq}\, \beta \cup \alpha$ for $\alpha \in H^p$, $\beta \in H^q$. For $p = q = 1$: $a \cup b = -b \cup a$.
$a \cup a \in H^2$: since $a \cup a = (-1)^{1\cdot 1}a \cup a = -a \cup a$, we get $2(a \cup a) = 0$. Over $\mathbb{Z}$ (which is torsion-free), $a \cup a = 0$. Similarly $b \cup b = 0$.
$a \cup b$: This is the fundamental class $[T^2]^* \in H^2(T^2)$ (up to sign), a generator of $H^2 = \mathbb{Z}$. It is non-zero.
The ring structure is therefore that of an exterior algebra: $H^*(T^2) \cong \Lambda_\mathbb{Z}[a, b]$, with $\mathbb{Z}$-basis $\{1, a, b, a \cup b\}$.
More generally, $H^*(T^n;\,\mathbb{Z}) \cong \Lambda_\mathbb{Z}[a_1, \ldots, a_n]$, the exterior algebra on $n$ generators of degree 1.
